commitMessage:
Impl #2402 - Migrate model processors from extension point to OSGi DS

Signed-off-by: Dirk Fauth <dirk.fauth@googlemail.com>
categoryReason: The change involves migrating existing classes to use OSGi Declarative Services by adding class-level and field-level annotations (`@Component`, `@Reference`) and implementing an interface (`IModelProcessorContribution`). This is a common migration pattern in Eclipse development.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently supports expression, statement, and annotation replacements, but not the addition of new class-level annotations, new fields, field-level annotations, or modifications to a class's `implements` clause. These are structural changes to class definitions that require more advanced AST manipulation capabilities.
summary: The commit migrates model processors to OSGi Declarative Services by adding `@Component` and `@Reference` annotations to classes and fields, and by implementing `IModelProcessorContribution`. These structural changes (adding annotations to classes/fields, adding new fields, and modifying the `implements` clause) are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. The DSL is designed for pattern matching and replacement of existing code elements, not for injecting new structural elements into a class.

commitMessage:
PropertyPageNode: log full exception if any instead of popup only

This issue did not show the root exception:
https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.debug/issues/570
categoryReason: The change involves specific Eclipse platform API usage (`StatusUtil`, `StatusManager`) for error handling and logging, which is distinct from general Java modernization, performance, or JUnit categories.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support bitwise operators (`|`, `&`, `^`, `~`, `>>`, `<<`) in replacement expressions. Specifically, supporting `|` for combining constant flags.
dslRuleAfterChange:
<!id: eclipse-log-full-exception>
<!description: Update StatusUtil.newStatus to include full status list and ensure StatusManager.handle logs exceptions.>
<!severity: info>
<!minJavaVersion: 9>
<!tags: eclipse-api, logging, error-handling>

org.eclipse.core.runtime.StatusUtil.newStatus($status, $msg) :: sourceVersionGE(9)
=> org.eclipse.core.runtime.StatusUtil.newStatus(java.util.List.of($status), $msg, null)
;;

org.eclipse.ui.statushandling.StatusManager.getManager().handle($errStatus, org.eclipse.ui.statushandling.StatusManager.SHOW)
=> org.eclipse.ui.statushandling.StatusManager.getManager().handle($errStatus, org.eclipse.ui.statushandling.StatusManager.SHOW | org.eclipse.ui.statushandling.StatusManager.LOG)
;;
summary: This commit modifies `StatusUtil.newStatus` to accept a `List<IStatus>` and an additional `null` argument, leveraging `List.of()` (Java 9+), and also adds `StatusManager.LOG` using a bitwise OR to `StatusManager.handle`. While the `StatusUtil.newStatus` part is generally implementable with a `sourceVersionGE(9)` guard, the addition of the bitwise OR operator (`|`) in the `StatusManager.handle` replacement is explicitly unsupported by the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, the transformation cannot be fully implemented as a DSL rule without a language extension for bitwise operators in replacements.

commitMessage:
CSS: Allow Color And Font Definitions To Be Marked As Not Editable

Color and Fonts can be defined as not editable if done via the
extensions point. Provide the same possibility if they are defined via
CSS.
irrelevantReason: The change involves adding new method declarations to existing classes (`setEditable(Boolean editable)` to `ColorDefinition` and `FontDefinition`). The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for matching and replacing existing code expressions or statements, not for generating new method bodies or altering class structure (e.g., adding methods or fields).
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require extensive language extensions to support adding new methods, fields, or modifying class structures. This goes beyond the current scope of expression/statement pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit adds a new method `setEditable(Boolean editable)` to two classes. The TriggerPattern DSL is limited to transforming existing expressions and statements and does not support structural changes like adding new methods to classes. Consequently, this change cannot be generalized into a DSL rule.
